title: Daily Briefing — Apr 22
headline: Tech leadership is holding, but greed is creeping in and defensive sectors are lagging—follow the flows, not the hype.
body: Right, big picture: SPY is hugging $704.08, still above its 20 MA ($672.06) and with VIX at 19.15, volatility is contracting. Tech remains the bid with XLK ($154.69) and DELL ripping +4.3%, but Fear & Greed has shot from 15.6 to 67.7 in a month—retail excitement piling in, check the receipts.

Sector rotation is clear: Industrials (XLI), Tech (XLK), and Real Estate (XLRE) are leading, but staples, healthcare, discretionary, and communication are lagging hard. Energy (XLE) is weakening, utilities (XLU) rolling over. Watch XLF ($52.30) as financials start improving—the canary isn't coughing yet, but if this turns, economy’s got the flu.

Contrarian warning: When defensive sectors lag, greed climbs, and headlines pump 'tech rally', you archive it—rotation risk is rising. Don’t chase moves like DELL or ARM at new highs; mean reversion zones are coming back into play. Remember: If it’s in the press, it’s in the price. Patience, react, don't predict.
callout_body:
SPY: $672.06 (20 MA)
QQQ: $604.06 (20 MA)
XLK: $141.33 (20 MA)
XLF: $50.51 (20 MA)
VIX: 19.15 (watch for spike)
Fear & Greed: 67.7 (Greed, but not extreme—monitor for >75 trigger)
DELL: $181.20 (20 MA, extended)
Watch staples (XLP $81.87), healthcare (XLV $146.87), and energy (XLE $58.52)—all approaching key MAs
action_items: If SPY, QQQ, XLK, or XLF retest 20 MA levels, use it as mean reversion entry zone—don’t chase.
Shift focus to early sector rotation: Look for laggards with improving momentum (Financials/XLF), but reduce exposure if Fear & Greed >75 or XLF breaks below $50.51.
closing_quote: Abundance mindset: If you missed the move, don’t chase, find the next opportunity.
